description: The lowest part of a building, the part that holds the weight; the base on which structure rests. 'Motha' names what holds all else—the hidden strength that enables everything above.
sound_symbolism: The word grounds deep. 'Mo' sets with heavy stability, base planted. Then 'tha' steadies with firm breath, the soft dental fricative anchoring the foundation in place.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'motha' (the foundation). Supports 'tomae' (floor), 'moru' (wall), 'toru' (roof). Often unseen but essential.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Foundations of practice—ethics, concentration, wisdom, the base supporting all development.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Foundation determines all—the base that must be right before building rises.
